data.text: There's already an `untrusted.go` in the evidence package. Let me examine it and understand the full picture.

data.output:
1	package evidence
2	
3	// Canonical boundary markers for prompt assembly when embedding
4	// untrusted artifact bodies.
5	const (
6		UntrustedDataOpen  = "<untrusted-data>"
7		UntrustedDataClose = "</untrusted-data>"
8	)
9	
10	// DelimitUntrustedData wraps body in a canonical untrusted-data envelope so
11	// prompt renderers can distinguish trusted instructions from artifact text.
12	func DelimitUntrustedData(body string) string {
13		return UntrustedDataOpen + "\n" + body + "\n" + UntrustedDataClose
14	}
15

data.output: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go:204:func TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted(t *testing.T) {

195			Title:       "overflow",
196			Description: strings.Repeat("D", 4000),
197			Acceptance:  "AC#1: anything",
198		}
199		res := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, 1, "revOF", "", t.TempDir(), nil, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
200		assert.True(t, res.Overflow,
201			"residual overflow after trimming must surface so callers can short-circuit")
202	}
203	
204	func TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted(t *testing.T) {
205		caps := testCaps()
206		root := t.TempDir()
207		docRel := "docs/body.md"
208		docAbs := filepath.Join(root, filepath.FromSlash(docRel))
209		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(docAbs), 0o755))
210		const payload = "artifact payload"
211		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(docAbs, []byte(payload), 0o644))
212	
213		diff := "diff --git a/notes.txt b/notes.txt\n@@ -1 +1 @@\n+" + payload + "\n"
214		b := makeBigBead()
215		refs := []GoverningRef{{ID: "FEAT-1", Path: docRel, Title: "Body"}}
216		res := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, 1, "rev-delim", diff, root, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
217	
218		docExpected := evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(payload)
219		diffExpected := evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(diff, "\n"))
220		assert.Contains(t, res.Prompt, docExpected,
221			"governing-doc artifact bodies must be wrapped in the canonical untrusted-data envelope")
222		assert.Contains(t, res.Prompt, diffExpected,
223			"diff artifact bodies must be wrapped in the canonical untrusted-data envelope")
224		assert.Contains(t, res.Prompt, evidence.UntrustedDataOpen)
225		assert.Contains(t, res.Prompt, evidence.UntrustedDataClose)
226		assert.Contains(t, res.Prompt, payload,
227			"the raw artifact payload must remain inside the wrapper")
228	}
229	
230	// TestReviewContextOverflow asserts the executor honors the pre-dispatch
231	// short-circuit: when bounded assembly cannot fit the prompt, no provider
232	// run happens and the surfaced error carries the literal substring
233	// 'context_overflow'.
234	func TestReviewContextOverflow(t *testing.T) {
235		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
236		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", projectRoot)
237		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
238		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
239	
240		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
241		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
242		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "README.md"), []byte("# x\n"), 0o644))
243		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
244			ID:          "ddx-overflow",
245			Title:       "context overflow",
246			Description: strings.Repeat("D", 4000),
247			Acceptance:  "AC#1",
248		}))
249		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", "README.md", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
250		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
251		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@example.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
252		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
253		headRaw, err := exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD").Output()
254		require.NoError(t, err)
